---
title: "principal_curvatures"
generated: true
description: "Per-vertex principal curvatures $( kappa_1,  kappa_2)$."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.geometry.surface`](../nitrix-geometry-surface.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:principal_curvatures -->
```python
principal_curvatures(mesh: Mesh, *, area_scheme: Literal['voronoi', 'barycentric'] = 'voronoi') -> Float[Array, 'n_vertices 2']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:principal_curvatures -->
Per-vertex principal curvatures $(\kappa_1, \kappa_2)$.

The two principal curvatures are ordered $\kappa_1 \geq \kappa_2$.
They follow from the mean and Gaussian curvatures via
$\kappa = H \pm \sqrt{\max(H^2 - K, 0)}$ (the discriminant is
clamped at zero, where it can dip slightly negative from discretisation).
Inherits the sign convention of [`mean_curvature`](../nitrix-geometry-surface/mean_curvature.qmd).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:principal_curvatures -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `mesh` | `Mesh` | — | — | Triangle mesh (closed). |
| `area_scheme` | `Literal['voronoi', 'barycentric']` | — | `'voronoi'` | Vertex-area scheme (`'voronoi'` default). |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `n_vertices` × `2`. Per-vertex principal curvatures, shape `(n_vertices, 2)`: column 0 is $\kappa_1$ (larger), column 1 is $\kappa_2$. Pure JAX; differentiable.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:principal_curvatures -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n_vertices` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `2` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
